steps.title: extractConstraints
steps.details: Extract travel constraints from user prompt
steps.output: Duration: 3 days, Budget: STANDARD, Style: LEISURE

steps.title: executeSearch
steps.output:
Provider: tavily, Query: Vienna
- Vienna - Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vienna): # Vienna. | Flag of Vienna Flag  Official seal of Vienna Seal  Coat of arms of Vienna Coat of arms  Official logo of Vienna Logo | |. | Vienna is located in Austria Vienna  Vienna  Location within Austria Show map of Austria  Vienna is located in Europe Vienna  Vienna  Location within Europe Show map of Europe | |. Vienna is located in Austria. Vienna is located in Europe. Vienna is the cultural, economic, and political centre of the country, the fifth-largest city by population in the European Union, and the most populous of the cities on the river Danube. The city lies on the eastern edge of the Vienna Woods (*Wienerwald*), the northeasternmost foothills of the Alps, that separate Vienna from the more western parts of Austria, at the transition to the Pannonian Basin. During the 16th century, the Habsburgs, who had succeeded the Babenbergs, established Vienna as the seat of the emperors of the Holy Roman Empire, a position it held until the empire's dissolution in 1806, with only a brief interruption.
- Vienna's online travel guide: the latest information and services ... (https://www.wien.info/en): Outdoor furniture at the MuseumsQuarter in Vienna. ### City oases in the open air. ### Explore Viennaâs Taverns and Beisl with ivie. # The official travel guide of the City of Vienna. ## ivie - The official city guide app. ivie, the digital city guide for Vienna, is your perfect companion. ### Summer in Vienna. That summer feeling is coming: summer in Vienna offers numerous outdoor activities, open-air events and cozy places to relax. Summer day on the Old Danube, boats in the water, Vienna skyline in the background. Two women walking in the city center of Vienna. An overview of Vienna's main sights from A to Z at a glance. ### ivie App. ivie is the digital city guide app for Vienna. The Vienna City Card is the perfect companion for exploring Vienna. Our event search feature presents you with a selection of events in Vienna. Tips for a visit to Vienna with the kids.
- A guide to Vienna for visitors (https://www.visitingvienna.com/visitorinfo/introduction-to-vienna): # A Guide to Vienna. This article is going to be biased because I live here and love the city. But let me just introduce you to a few topics that should help you find your way around Vienna (literally and figuratively) as you consider or plan a visit. Weâll look (briefly) at what the city offers visitors, its history, its layout, and some basic information on issues like public transport and languages. + What to do in a day. + What to do in the evening. This really is a beautiful clean city full of urban charm, with a rich and long history, lots of culture, plenty to see and do (also with children), excellent services and amenities, and a selection of cakes that will do evil things to your willpower. Many people choose to visit the landmarks associated with the famous composers, such as their homes, or listen to their works in one of the cityâs (often historical) concert venues: Vienna has one of the worldâs greatest opera houses and one of itâs best classical music concert halls.
- Vienna | History, Population, & Facts | Britannica (https://www.britannica.com/place/Vienna): Neptune's Fountain (foreground) and the Gloriette, on the grounds of Schloss SchÃ¶nbrunn, Vienna. # Vienna. Encyclopaedia Britannica's editors oversee subject areas in which they have extensive knowledge, whether from years of experience gained by working on that content or via study for an advanced degree.... * What is Vienna known for? * What are some famous landmarks or buildings in Vienna? In 1955 the State Treaty, by which the country regained independence, was signed with the four occupying powers, and Vienna became once again the capital of a sovereign Austria. Observe Vienna's Schloss SchÃ¶nbrunn, Gothic St. Stephen's Cathedral, and postmodern Haas Haus buildingOnce the seat of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, Vienna is filled with majestic sights, both old and new. It is still possible to live in Vienna at almost the same pace and in much the same style as it was a century ago. Tour Vienna's Rococo, Gothic, and postmodern buildings and learn about the city's imperial pastAn introduction to Vienna, Austria.
